seo.title: AI Agent Authentication and Authorization in Flowise Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent authentication and authorization in Flowise agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Flowise, AI Agent Authentication and Authorization issues manifest when chatflows expose sensitive tools, vector stores, or API endpoints without proper user/session validation, allowing unauthorized access to premium features or private data. Drag-and-drop nodes like Custom Tool, Agent, and Vector Store Retrieval often lack built-in auth guards, leading to privilege escalation across multi-agent handoffs. This is exacerbated by Flowise's stateless chatflow execution where context like user tokens isn't propagated between nodes.

howItManifests.scenario: Custom Tool Node with Database Access
howItManifests.symptom: Tool executes SQL queries for any chat input without checking user permissions
howItManifests.impact: Attackers extract sensitive customer data via crafted prompts

howItManifests.symptom: Downstream agents inherit elevated privileges from upstream admin tools
howItManifests.impact: Privilege escalation across agent handoffs exposes admin functions

howItManifests.symptom: Returns private enterprise documents to unauthenticated users
howItManifests.impact: Data leakage of proprietary information through RAG pipelines

howItManifests.symptom: Public chatflow endpoint bypasses session authentication
howItManifests.impact: Anyone with chatflow ID can trigger expensive/premium operations

detection.name: Scan Custom Tool Nodes for Auth Checks
detection.description: Detect tools missing user context validation before executing sensitive operations

detection.name: Trace Context Propagation Across Agents
detection.description: Verify user context flows through multi-agent handoffs without privilege escalation

detection.name: Audit Vector Store Access Controls

fixes.name: Add User Context to Custom Tool
fixes.description: Inject user session data into tool execution context

fixes.name: Implement Agent Context Propagation
fixes.description: Ensure user context flows through multi-agent chains

fixes.name: Secure Vector Store with Tenant Filter
fixes.description: Add user-specific filtering to document retrieval

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Authentication and Authorization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly into Flowise chatflows as a callback handler, automatically tracing authentication flows across all nodes and detecting missing permission checks in real-time. During chatflow execution, Vex monitors user context propagation, tool privilege escalation, and vector store access patterns, flagging violations before they impact production.

Vex's policy engine validates that every sensitive operation (database calls, API requests, document retrieval) receives proper user context injection. It generates remediation blueprints showing exactly which nodes need auth guards and provides one-click fixes via Flowise's node configuration API.

The integration requires adding Vex as a global callback in your Flowise deployment, after which it passively monitors all chatflows while providing API endpoints for automated remediation workflows.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one callback handler to secure all your Flowise agents against auth failures.
